name: Select commodity to test
pageTitle: Choose Commodity To Test - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057 - V1
heading: CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057 - V1 Laboratory tests
secondaryActions: Save and set as in progress

secondaryActionNotes.Cancel: Cancel destination is source-conditional (MISSED BY TRACES — corpus only ever had source=notests): when source=='notests' the link returns to /protected/bip-notifications/{referenceNumber}/hub; otherwise it returns to .../lab-test/lab-tests-to-be-performed (chooseCommodity.html:48-55). Confidence: legacy.
secondaryActionNotes.Save and set as in progress: The <form method=POST action=choose-commodity> exists solely to carry this status-bar button; the choose_commodity POST handler is a no-op (choose_commodity.js:32) whose only effect is setInProgressTransformer.updateNotificationStatusToInProgress (choose_commodity.js:36). The commodity choice itself is a GET link, not this POST. Confidence: legacy.

structure.govukComponent: (non-standard: .heading-tertiary span nested inside the H1, not a govuk-caption-xl)

structure.text: Columns: Commodity code | Description | Species | (visually-hidden 'Select'). One row per commodity, each with a 'Select' action link.

govukComponents.usage: Lists the notification's commodities (code, description, species) with a per-row 'Select' link so the inspector picks which commodity line to sample/test. aria-label="select the commodity sampled table".
govuk-heading-xl for the page H1 (reference + 'Laboratory tests'); govuk-heading-m for the H2 'Select the commodity sampled'.

Per-row 'Select' action links (id=choose-commodity-N) that navigate to the choose-lab-test page for that commodity index; plus the 'Cancel' link (returns to the CHED hub). The 'Back' link is present as a top-of-page nav link.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 125 (link resolved to Select)

govukComponents.usage: 'Save and set as in progress' button in the decision status bar above the main content (page-level workflow control, not part of the commodity-select form).
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 125 (a11y snapshot, button ref=e62)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: <span class="heading-tertiary"> nested inside the <h1 class="govuk-heading-xl">, containing <span id="reference-number"> and <span id="cved-version-number">
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the CHED reference number and version (e.g. 'CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057 - V1') as a caption-like prefix inside the H1, followed by the page title span (id=page-primary-title, 'Laboratory tests').
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The GOV.UK caption pattern is a separate govuk-caption-xl/-l element placed immediately before the H1, not a custom span nested inside it. IPAFFS folds the reference number into the H1 with a bespoke .heading-tertiary class. In the rebuild, use govuk-caption-xl above a plain H1.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: commodity-code / description / species (custom classes on <th class="govuk-table__header ...">)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke CSS classes on the table column headers, presumably for column widths/styling of the Commodity code, Description and Species columns.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom CSS layered onto govuk-table headers rather than using govuk width override utilities. Minor; the govuk-table itself is standard. In the rebuild, drop these in favour of govuk-!-width-* utility classes or a plain govuk-table if column sizing is not needed.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-!-width-one-quarter / govuk-!-width-one-half utility classes on govuk-table__header

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: change (custom class on the action <td class="govuk-table__cell change">)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke class on the cell holding the per-row 'Select' link.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Cosmetic custom class on an otherwise standard govuk-table cell. No functional concern; drop in the rebuild.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Row-selection via per-row 'Select' links instead of a form control
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Each commodity row carries a govuk-link 'Select' (id=choose-commodity-N) that GETs the next page for that commodity index. There is no radio group and no primary submit button for the choice — clicking a link IS the selection.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is a link-driven selection, not a form submission. It is an accepted GOV.UK table-action pattern, but if the new app wants a single-select-then-continue flow it could instead render radios + a Continue button. Worth a design decision at rebuild.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-radios (one radio per commodity) + govuk-button 'Continue' — or keep the table-with-action-link pattern.

fields.name: choose-commodity-{index}
fields.label: Select the commodity sampled
fields.hint: Rendered as a table (aria-label "select the commodity sampled table") with one row per commodity. Columns: Commodity code, Description, Species. The inspector picks a commodity by clicking that row's 'Select' link (id=choose-commodity-N), which carries the commodity index into the URL — there is no radio group or submit button.
fields.controlNote: Spec previously carried control='radio' (template default). Corrected to 'action-link': the rendered trace shows a govuk-link 'Select' per row and no radio input, and the page object confirms the same — LabTestsCommoditySelectPage exposes only linkSelect = getByRole('link', { name: 'Select' }) (page-objects/decision/LabTestsCommoditySelectPage.ts:6-8), with the workflow driving it via labTestsCommoditySelect.linkSelect.click() (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:417). The govuk-radios alternative is captured in nonStandardPatterns as a rebuild decision, not as observed behaviour.
fields.conditionalOn: Post-submission inspector laboratory-tests flow, reached from the Decision Hub 'Laboratory tests' link then the Lab tests intro page (radio Yes → this commodity-select page; the intro also offers No and Random — LabTestsIntroPage.ts:6-16 / ched-d-workflows.ts:410-417). The row list is the commodities on this CHED-D notification, so the row count == number of commodity lines. Clicking a row's Select link leads to the Lab tests commodity-test page (search + choose-laboratory-test-N — LabTestsCommodityTestPage.ts).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 125; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 130 (both show a single row 10064000 Broken rice). Link pattern + selection corroborated (inferred) by page-objects/decision/LabTestsCommoditySelectPage.ts:6-8 and workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:417. Value corroborated (inferred) by types/commodity-code.ts (brokenRice='10064000') and ched-d-workflows.ts DEFAULT_CONFIG (commodityCode: commodityCode.brokenRice), which is why the whole corpus shows exactly one row.

legacyEnrichment.source: The lab-test flow renders from ipaffs-frontend-decision (the /decision/vnet host), not ipaffs-frontend-notification. Template: service/src/views/bip/chooseCommodity.html; handler: service/src/routes/handlers/bip/choose_commodity.js.
legacyEnrichment.rowSource: Rows come from partOne.commodities.commodityComplement (choose_commodity.js:17). Per-row cells are commodityID, commodityDescription and speciesName (chooseCommodity.html:35-37). The Select link href is /protected/bip-notifications/{referenceNumber}/lab-test/{@index}/choose-lab-test?source={source}, id=choose-commodity-{@index} (chooseCommodity.html:39-41) — @index is the commodityComplement index, corroborating the confirmed id=choose-commodity-N pattern.
legacyEnrichment.woodPackagingExclusion: MISSED BY TRACES: the row loop is wrapped in {{^if commodityComplement.isWoodPackaging}} (chooseCommodity.html:33), so commodity lines flagged isWoodPackaging are NOT offered for lab-test selection — the same filter used on the review/summary transformers (notification.js:249-254, commodities_summary.js:7). The single-row broken-rice corpus never exercised this; a notification with a wood-packaging commodity line would show that line hidden from this table. Confidence: legacy (chooseCommodity.html:33).

fields.name: commodity-code (table column)
fields.label: Commodity code
fields.hint: Read-only table column showing the commodity/CN code for each notification line (e.g. 10064000).

fields.name: description (table column)
fields.label: Description
fields.hint: Read-only table column showing the commodity description (e.g. Broken rice).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 125 (th description; cell 'Broken rice')

fields.name: species (table column)
fields.label: Species
fields.hint: Read-only table column for species. Empty in both observed CHED-D traces (species applies to CHED-A/CHED-P animal commodities, not CHED-D high-risk food/feed of non-animal origin).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 125 (th species; cell empty). Corroborated empty in trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 130.
fields.legacyNote: Column value is bound to commodityComplement.speciesName (chooseCommodity.html:37). For CHED-D commodities speciesName is typically unset, hence the observed blank cell; the column is shared with the animal CHED types where it is populated. Confidence: legacy (chooseCommodity.html:37).

fields.name: source
fields.label: (hidden) source
fields.hint: Hidden input carrying the ?source query value into the form and into every per-row Select link (chooseCommodity.html:12, :41). Only observed value is 'notests' (constants.js:195 NOTESTS_QUERY_VALUE). Set when the inspector reaches commodity-select from the 'no lab tests recorded yet' state — the hub 'Laboratory tests' link routes here with ?source=notests when partTwo.laboratoryTests.singleLaboratoryTests is not yet completed (lab_tests_route_helper.js:8-15). It also switches the Cancel destination (see secondaryActions).
fields.evidence: chooseCommodity.html:12,41,48; choose_commodity.js:18-19; constants.js:192,195; lab_tests_route_helper.js:6,14. MISSED BY TRACES: rendered as a hidden input, never surfaced in a snapshot.
fields.note: Optional, not mandatory — the handler reads it via _.get(request,'query.source') and tolerates its absence (choose_commodity.js:19; sourceIfAvailableInRequest returns '' when unset, choose_commodity.js:25-28). Legacy behaviour; the rebuild may model arrival-state differently.

fields.name: crumb
fields.label: (hidden) crumb
fields.hint: Hidden CSRF token input (chooseCommodity.html:10). Present because the surrounding <form method=POST> exists to carry the 'Save and set as in progress' status action; the commodity choice itself is a GET link, not this POST.
fields.evidence: chooseCommodity.html:10. MISSED BY TRACES: hidden input. Requiredness is framework CSRF, not a CHED-D validation group — always present on the POST form.

fields.name: etag
fields.label: (hidden) etag
fields.hint: Hidden optimistic-concurrency token for the notification, echoed back on the status-update POST (chooseCommodity.html:11).
fields.evidence: chooseCommodity.html:11. MISSED BY TRACES: hidden input. Requiredness is concurrency-control, not a CHED-D validation group.

validationMessagesNote: Empty is now LEGACY-CONFIRMED, not merely unrendered: the POST route handler is a no-op (choose_commodity.js:32) with no Joi validate options on the route (routes.js:328-333), and the commodity choice is a GET action-link. There is no user-submitted field on this page to validate, so IPAFFS has no ValidationMessages.properties entry for it. If the rebuild switches to radios + Continue, a 'select a commodity' error would be a NEW message with no legacy source (gap). Confidence: legacy (choose_commodity.js:32; routes.js:322-333).
openQuestions: Both observed CHED-D notifications had exactly ONE commodity (10064000 Broken rice), so multi-row behaviour (pagination, sorting, multiple species) is a gap — never exercised in the CHED-D corpus. The single row is explained by the test harness: types/commodity-code.ts brokenRice='10064000' and ched-d-workflows.ts DEFAULT_CONFIG adds one commodity (commodityCode.brokenRice), so no CHED-D test ever built a multi-commodity notification. A real CHED-D with multiple commodity lines would render multiple rows, each with its own 'Select' link (id=choose-commodity-0..N).

The Species column was empty in every CHED-D trace. Confirm whether Species is ever populated for CHED-D, or whether the column is inherited from a shared template used by CHED-A/CHED-P and is always blank for CHED-D (high-risk food/feed of non-animal origin). No CHED-D test drives or asserts a Species value here.

No validation was observed on this page (it is a link-driven selection, not a form submit), so there is no error copy — and no CHED-D test (tests/notification/ched-d/*.spec.ts) or the CHED-D accessibility test asserts any message on this page. validationMessages stays empty (gap). If the rebuild uses radios + Continue, an error message for 'no commodity selected' would be new and is a gap.

Selection is via per-row 'Select' links (GET), not a form control — confirmed by trace and corroborated by the page object (LabTestsCommoditySelectPage.ts:6-8) which exposes only linkSelect. Rebuild decision needed: keep the table-with-action-link pattern or switch to govuk-radios + Continue.

URL carries ?source=notests. RESOLVED (legacy): 'notests' is the only defined source value (constants.js:195 NOTESTS_QUERY_VALUE) and is set when the hub 'Laboratory tests' link routes here in the not-yet-completed lab-tests state (lab_tests_route_helper.js:8-15). It flows through a hidden input into every Select link and flips the Cancel destination (notests→hub, else→lab-tests-to-be-performed; chooseCommodity.html:48-55). No other source value exists in the decision service, so 'other source values' is a closed question, not a gap. The rebuild may model arrival-state differently (policy, not copy).

Wood-packaging commodity lines are hidden from this table (legacy: chooseCommodity.html:33 {{^if commodityComplement.isWoodPackaging}}). Never exercised in the single-row broken-rice corpus. If the rebuild reuses the same commodity list for lab-test selection it must decide whether to inherit this exclusion (wood packaging is not lab-testable in the legacy model) or list all lines.

The Lab tests intro page that precedes this page offers three options — Yes, No, Random (LabTestsIntroPage.ts:6-16). Only the Yes → commodity-select path is exercised by the CHED-D happy-path workflow. Whether 'Random' routes to this same commodity-select page or elsewhere is a gap. (There is also a separate LabTestsReasonPage with a 'Suspicion' radio — LabTestsReasonPage.ts — not observed in the CHED-D corpus; its position relative to this page is a gap.)
